steps.title: Identify the hypotenuse and legs
steps.content: The figure is a right triangle. The legs are $4$ and $b$, and the hypotenuse is $19$.

steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
For a right triangle, the sum of the squares of the legs equals the square of the hypotenuse:
$${4}^{2}+{b}^{2}={19}^{2}$$

steps.title: Eliminate the other equations
steps.content:
• $4b=19$: treats the relationship as a product, which has no geometric basis here. ✗
  • $4+b=19$: treats the relationship as a sum of sides, not the Pythagorean theorem. ✗
  • ${4}^{2}+{b}^{2}={19}^{2}$: matches the Pythagorean relationship. ✓
  • ${4}^{2}-{b}^{2}={19}^{2}$: subtracts the leg squares instead of adding them. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
